mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and profit margin compression.
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS declines of >100k persons fail to produce negative 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, reducing equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower expected cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.08% indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in >50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer demand and construction activity that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 626833.0 million dollars, HOUST at 1679.0 thousand units
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profitability and equity returns over 5 trading days as market participants reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines fail to produce negative 5-day returns in >52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity risk-off behavior and negative forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate recession.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases >0.3pp in 3 months fail to produce negative 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary policy tightness that erodes real equity returns and increases discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the Fed maintains restrictive stance.
failure_condition: Joint condition of elevated FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative 60-day returns in >55% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter expansion above 2.5% annualized rate signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 19478.893 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth >2.5% annualized fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, it signals stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations through simultaneous earnings headwinds and discount rate increases, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline AND UNRATE rises >0.2pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise fails to produce negative 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
